mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window, or forward returns are positive in >55% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 4.5% threshold, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable and non-durable goods, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, leading to short-term positive equity returns as markets reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during tightening periods, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year window, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns in >55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices forces markets to anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates creates a favorable macro environment where corporate earnings expand without facing higher financing costs, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive significance in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative in >50% of high-GDP/low-rate quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 1.4 million units signals robust construction sector activity and strong household balance sheets, which translates into higher capital goods demand and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of months where HOUST exceeds 1400, or the threshold loses predictive power after adjusting for regional construction cost inflation.
